methods.name: Stage-Progression

data.narrative:
Manna Aero is a Series B drone and autonomous systems company that has raised $110M to date. The valuation universe for Drones & Autonomous comprises 7 private peers including Elroy Air, Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Asylon Robotics, with no public comparables available.

Stage-progression methodology is the only applicable triangulation approach given data constraints. Using total raised ($110M) as a valuation proxy and applying a typical Series B to next-stage step-up multiplier of 1.9x yields an implied valuation of $209M. A peer-median approach was not viable; while 4 same-sector peers exist, none of the narrow filters (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation tier) produced the minimum 3 datapoints required for robust comparison. Relying on an all-sector median would introduce material bias given heterogeneity within Drones & Autonomous.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $178M to $240M, with a median point estimate of $209M. This range reflects ±15% sensitivity around the stage-progression result. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 1.9x multiplier for Manna Aero specifically—actual step-up multiples vary materially by market conditions, unit economics, and capital efficiency—and the absence of publicly traded benchmarks limits downside and upside calibration. Investors should weight this single-method conclusion accordingly and supplement with detailed operational due diligence on burn rate and path to profitability.
